返回本批次

Apple App Store 上的假冒 Ledger 钱包应用程序导致用户损失 950 万美元

Users lose $9.5M to fake Ledger wallet app on the Apple App Store

www.web3isgoinggreat.com 作者 CharlesW 分数 70 评论 44 2026/04/16 02:35
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 2026 年 4 月 13 日 Ledger 加密货币钱包应用程序的假冒版本进入通常精心策划的 Apple 应用商店后，客户因该恶意产品损失了 950 万美元。人们相信这是一款真正的 Ledger 产品，于是在应用程序中输入助记词，然后发现他们的钱包立即被掏空。一位名为 G. Love 的音乐家受害者写道：“当我将 Ledger 切换到新电脑时，我不小心从苹果商店下载了恶意分类账应用程序，我的所有 BTC 瞬间就消失了。”据他称，他损失了 5.9 BTC（约合 445,000 美元）。 加密货币侦探 zachxbt 通过 Kucoin 追查了部分被盗资金。 Kucoin 是一家中国加密货币交易所，最近因许可和反洗钱失败而被罚款并被迫退出美国市场。他写道：“三个最大的受害者各损失了七位数。”苹果公司于 4 月 13 日从 App Store 中删除了该恶意应用程序，即该应用程序添加六天后。 “Apple App Store 上的一个假 Ledger 应用程序耗尽了 950 万美元的加密货币”，CoinDesk [存档]zachxbt 的 Telegram 帖子 [存档]G. Love 的推文帖子 [存档]2026 年 4 月 13 日愚人节黑客笑话两周后，Hyperbridge 被利用愚人节当天，Hyperbridge 区块链桥项目发布了一条推文，声称朝鲜 Lazarus 黑客组织已经耗尽了加密货币该项目 3700 万美元。链接的博客文章包含 Rickroll GIF 和“为什么 Hyperbridge 无法被黑客攻击”的解释。第二天，Hyperbridge 开发人员发布了区块链交易的屏幕截图，并写道“uniBTC 开发者 Lmao 正在测试 Hyperbridge。我希望你有一台量子计算机兄弟”。另一位评论者回答说，“规则#1不要主动激怒攻击者”。大约两周后，攻击者能够伪造一项交易来更改 Polkadot/以太坊桥合约的管理员权限，从而允许他们铸造 10 亿个 DOT 代币。由于流动性有限，他们能够兑现约 237,000 美元。愚人节的帖子已被删除。“Hyperbridge 在愚人节黑客恶作剧后不到两周的时间内利用了这一点”，Protos [存档]Tweet by 0xMarforio [存档]2026 年 4 月 6 日比特币 Depot 被黑客攻击价值 367 万美元比特币 ATM 运营商 Bitcoin Depot 披露了 3 月 23 日的黑客攻击事件攻击者从公司钱包中窃取了 50.903 BTC（约合 367 万美元）。根据该公司向 SEC 披露的信息，攻击者获得了该公司 IT 系统和钱包凭证的访问权限，从而窃取了资产。Bitcoin Depot 是全球和美国最大的加密 ATM 运营商，在美国拥有约 8,700 个自助终端，在全球拥有 9,200 个。Bitcoin Depot Inc. 于 2026 年 4 月 6 日提交的 SEC 8-K 表格顶级加密 ATM 运营商、Coin ATM Radar4 月2026 年 1 月 1 日，Drift 被利用了 2.85 亿美元位于 Solana 的 Drift defi 永续期货交易所被利用了 2.85 亿美元。该项目在社交媒体上向社区发出警报，写道：“Drift Protocol 正在遭受主动攻击......这不是愚人节玩笑。”该项目后来将该漏洞描述为“涉及持久随机数的新颖攻击，导致 Drift 的安全委员会管理权力迅速接管。”一旦攻击者获得了管理功能，他们就会迅速消除协议的风险管理限制，并耗尽大量代币，然后将其交换为 USDC，然后交换为 ETH。这次攻击归因于极其复杂的社会工程，很可能是朝鲜黑客所为。一些人批评 USDC 的发行人 Circle 没有在 USDC 持有的被盗资金的六个小时内冻结这些资金。与 ETH 不同，USDC 由一家中心化公司控制，该公司可以而且经常冻结被盗或与非法活动有关的资产。这次盗窃是 defi 历史上最大的盗窃案之一。“Drift 称 2.8 亿美元的利用与‘复杂’的管理员接管有关；ZachXBT 批评 Circle 的 USDC 处理方式”，Drift Protocol 的 BlockTweet Drift Protocol 的推文主题2026 年 3 月 24 日Moonwell 面临100 万美元的治理攻击 Moonwell 借贷协议在其已弃用的 Moonriver 实例上面临治理攻击，这可能导致该项目损失 100 万美元。由于 Moonwell 的 MFAM 治理代币交易价格不到一美分，攻击者能够积累约 4000 万个代币，提交恶意提案并达到法定人数。 Moonwell 治理代币持有者在 3 月 27 日投票结束前争先恐后地投票否决了该提案。最终，面对投票失败，攻击者抛售了自己持有的 MFAM，提案因余额低于提案阈值而被取消。这只是 Moonwell 最近遇到的麻烦，此前该协议在 2 月份因预言机配置错误而损失了 178 万美元，并在 2025 年 11 月损失了 370 万美元。提案，Moonwell 治理 [存档]Blockful 的推文帖子 [存档]2026 年 3 月 24 日BalancerLabs 在遭受 1.1 亿美元的黑客攻击后关闭 2025 年 11 月的一次攻击导致 Balancer defi 协议损失了 1.1 亿美元，该项目背后的公司宣布将关闭。除了巨大损失之外，此次黑客攻击还导致用户逃离协议，Balancer 锁定的总价值迅速从 7.75 亿美元左右暴跌至 3 亿美元左右。此后，该协议持续下跌，目前徘徊在 1.5 亿美元左右。Balancer 联合创始人 Fernando Martinelli 表示，他强烈考虑完全关闭该协议，但最终决定继续该项目，因为该项目产生的收入相对较少。相反，该项目将转向由 DAO 和运营公司运营，Martinelli 希望这将使他们能够避开“真实且持续的法律风险”和“过去安全事件的责任”。尽管另一位 Balancer 联合创始人乐观地将此视为 Balancer“更好篇章的开始”，但骨干人员是否能够重振该项目仍有待观察。“关于 Balancer 的未来：关闭 Balancer 实验室，支持前进之路”，Balancer 治理论坛 [存档]Marcus Hardt 的推文2026 年 3 月 22 日USR 稳定币因 2400 万美元的利用而被利用 Resolv USD 稳定币（也称为 USR）在利用者能够铸造和出售数千万个无支持的代币后，失去了其预期的美元挂钩并跌至 0.14 美元左右。 USR 是一种资产支持的稳定币，使用比特币、ETH 和其他稳定币等加密资产作为抵押品。 剥削者利用 USR 铸币代码中的缺陷创建了数千万个 USR 代币，而无需存入任何资产来支持它们。然后，攻击者出售了无支持的 USR，使稳定币的价格跌至 0.14 美元。攻击者已获利至少 11,400 ETH（约合 2400 万美元），尽管他们仍在出售。一些 defi 协议暂停了 USR 暴露的策略，以避免下游影响。 Resolv 发表声明称，该代币的抵押品池不受影响，但这对于那些购买无支持的 USR 的人来说可能不太安慰。“Resolv Labs 的稳定币因攻击者铸造数百万代币而脱钩”，Cointelegraph [存档]Resolv Labs 的推文 [存档]2026 年 3 月 17 日，金星协议在利用 BNB 链的金星协议借贷后累积了 215 万美元的坏账在一名利用者操纵 Thena 协议的 THE 代币价格后，该协议累积了 215 万美元的坏账。 THE 的流动性非常低，剥削者利用它来操纵 THE 价格预言机，通过以 THE 为抵押借款，用借来的资金购买更多的 THE，如此重复，导致价格预言机反映的价格越来越高。攻击者能够通过“捐赠”资金而不是以标准方式存入资金来避免 Venus 的供应上限。虽然该漏洞使 Venus 协议背负了超过 200 万美元的坏账，但尚不清楚攻击者是否从该漏洞中获利。剥削者的头寸最终被清算，导致价格上涨崩溃。然而，剥削者也有可能利用其他地方的价格差异来获利。Venus Protocol 过去曾遇到过许多问题，尤其是在 2023 年 6 月，当时一名小偷在 Venus 上用被盗代币借了 1.5 亿美元，然后面临清算，因此开发 BNB 链的团队不得不进行干预。“在利用操纵 Thena 代币价格后，Venus Protocol 留下了大约 200 万美元的坏账”， 2026年3月15日BlockFills破产在停止存款和取款大约一个月后，美国加密货币贷款机构BlockFills以流动性问题和“最近的市场和财务状况”为由申请破产。特拉华州破产法院提交的文件显示，该公司拥有 5000 万至 1 亿美元的资产和 1 亿至 5 亿美元的负债。债权人名单中包括 007 Capital 和 Artha Investment Partners 等客户，该公司还向加密货币贷款机构 Nexo 支付了 475 万美元的未偿还贷款。债权人名单上还有芝加哥黑鹰队，BlockFills 于 2022 年与该队签署了赞助协议。BlockFills 得到了包括 Susquehanna 和 CME Ventures 在内的投资者的支持。第 11 章自愿请愿书，Reliz Technology Group Holdings Inc.2026 年 3 月 12 日，交易者在 Aave 互换中损失了近 5000 万美元，出了问题使用 Aave 界面的交易者试图将 5000 万美元 USDT 兑换为亚夫。然而，由于订单规模巨大，此次采购对平均价格产生了巨大影响。 Aave 界面向客户发出有关价格影响的警告，交易者单击复选框以接受订单条款。最终，他们只收到了 324 AAVE（约 37,600 美元）作为 5000 万美元的回报，在此过程中损失了 99.9% 的资产。Aave 创始人提出退还用户从交易中收取的 60 万美元费用，并承认“还有额外的费用”。行业可以构建护栏以更好地保护用户”。Stani Kulechov 的推文 [存档]Martin Grabina 的推文 [存档]没有 JavaScript？这也很酷！查看该网站的 Web 1.0 版本以查看更多条目。

Original Summary

  1. April 13, 2026After a fake version of the Ledger cryptocurrency wallet app made it onto the normally highly curated Apple App store, customers lost $9.5 million dollars to the malicious product. Believing it was a genuine Ledger product, people entered their seed phrases into the app, then discovered their wallets were immediately drained.One victim, a musician who goes by G. Love, wrote: "I lost my retirement fund in a hack/Scam when I switched my Ledger over to my new computer and by accident downloaded a malicious ledger app from the Apple store. All my BTC gone in an instant." According to him, he lost 5.9 BTC (~$445,000).Crypto sleuth zachxbt traced some of the stolen funds through Kucoin, a Chinese cryptocurrency exchange that was recently fined and forced to exit US markets over licensing and anti-money laundering failures. "The three largest victims lost seven figures each," he wrote.Apple removed the malicious app from their App Store on April 13, six days after it had been added. "A fake Ledger app on the Apple App Store drained $9.5 million in crypto", CoinDesk [archive]Telegram post by zachxbt [archive]Tweet thread by G. Love [archive]April 13, 2026Hyperbridge exploited two weeks after April Fools' hack jokeOn April Fools' Day, the Hyperbridge blockchain bridge project posted a tweet claiming that the North Korean Lazarus hacking group had drained $37 million from the project. A linked blog post contained a Rickroll GIF and an explanation of "Why Hyperbridge can't be hacked".The following day, a Hyperbridge developer posted a screenshot of a blockchain transaction, writing "Lmao the uniBTC exploiter is testing Hyperbridge. I hope you have a quantum computer bro". Another commenter replied, "Rule #1 dont actively provoke attackers".About two weeks later, an attacker was able to forge a transaction to change the admin rights for the Polkadot/Ethereum bridge contract, allowing them to mint 1 billion DOT tokens. They were able to cash out about $237,000 due to limited liquidity.The April Fools' posts have since been deleted."Hyperbridge exploited less than two weeks after April Fools’ day hack prank", Protos [archive]Tweet by 0xMarforio [archive]April 6, 2026Bitcoin Depot hacked for $3.67 millionBitcoin ATM operator Bitcoin Depot has disclosed a March 23 hack in which attackers stole 50.903 BTC (~$3.67 million) from company wallets. According to the company's disclosure with the SEC, the exploiters gained access to the company's IT systems and wallet credentials, allowing them to steal the assets.Bitcoin Depot is the largest operator of crypto ATMs globally and in the United States, with approximately 8,700 kiosks in the US and 9,200 worldwide.SEC Form 8-K filed by Bitcoin Depot Inc. on April 6, 2026Top Crypto ATM Operators, Coin ATM RadarApril 1, 2026Drift exploited for $285 millionThe Solana-based Drift defi perpetual futures exchange was exploited for $285 million. The project alerted the community on social media, writing: "Drift Protocol is experiencing an active attack. ... This is not an April Fools joke."The project later described the exploit as "a novel attack involving durable nonces, resulting in a rapid takeover of Drift's Security Council administrative powers." Once the attacker had access to admin capabilities, they quickly eliminated risk management limits on the protocol and drained huge quantities of tokens, which they swapped to USDC and then ETH. The attack was attributed to extremely sophisticated social engineering, likely by North Korean hackers.Some have criticized USDC's issuer, Circle, for not freezing the stolen funds during the six hours they were held in USDC. Unlike ETH, USDC is controlled by a centralized company that can, and regularly does, freeze assets determined to have been stolen or connected to illicit activity.The theft is among the largest in defi history."Drift says $280M exploit tied to 'sophisticated' admin takeover; ZachXBT criticizes Circle over USDC handling", The BlockTweet by Drift ProtocolTweet thread by Drift ProtocolMarch 24, 2026Moonwell faces $1 million governance attackThe Moonwell lending protocol faced a governance attack on its deprecated Moonriver instance that could have drained $1 million from the project. Because Moonwell's MFAM governance token trades at fractions of a cent, an attacker was able to accumulate around 40 million tokens, submit a malicious proposal, and achieve quorum. Moonwell governance token holders scrambled to vote down the proposal before the voting ended on March 27.Ultimately, facing being outvoted, the attacker dumped their MFAM holdings and the proposal was canceled as their balance had fallen below the proposal threshold.This was only the most recent of Moonwell's troubles after the protocol suffered a $1.78 million loss in February due to an oracle misconfiguration and a $3.7 million loss in November 2025.Attack proposal, Moonwell governance [archive]Tweet thread by Blockful [archive]March 24, 2026Balancer Labs shuts down after $110 million hackAfter a November 2025 exploit in which $110 million was drained from the Balancer defi protocol, the company behind the project has announced it will shut down. Besides the massive loss, the hack also caused users to flee the protocol, and Balancer's total value locked quickly plummeted from around $775 million to around $300 million. It has continued to decline since, now hovering around $150 million.Balancer co-founder Fernando Martinelli has said he strongly considered shutting down the protocol entirely, but ultimately decided to continue the project as it generates a relatively small amount of revenue. Instead, the project will move to being operated by a DAO and operating company, which Martinelli hopes will allow them to dodge "real and ongoing legal exposure" and "the liability of past security incidents".Although another Balancer co-founder has optimistically presented this as "the start of a better chapter" for Balancer, it remains to be seen whether a skeleton crew will be able to revive the project."On the Future of Balancer: Shutting Down Balancer Labs, Supporting the Path Forward", Balancer governance forum [archive]Tweet by Marcus HardtMarch 22, 2026USR stablecoin depegs in $24 million exploitThe Resolv USD stablecoin, also known as USR, lost its intended dollar peg and dropped to around $0.14 after an exploiter was able to mint and sell tens of millions of unbacked tokens. USR is an asset-backed stablecoin that uses cryptoassets like bitcoin, ETH, and other stablecoins as collateral.An exploiter took advantage of a flaw in USR's minting code to create tens of millions of USR tokens without depositing any assets to back them. The attacker then sold the unbacked USR, crashing the stablecoin's price to as low as $0.14. The attacker has profited at least 11,400 ETH (~$24 million), though they are still selling.Some defi protocols paused USR-exposed strategies to avoid downstream impacts. Resolv issued a statement that the token's collateral pool was unaffected, though this is likely little comfort for those who purchased the unbacked USR."Resolv Labs’ stablecoin depegs as attacker mints millions of tokens", Cointelegraph [archive]Tweet by Resolv Labs [archive]March 17, 2026Venus Protocol accumulates $2.15 million in bad debt after exploitThe BNB Chain's Venus Protocol lending protocol accumulated $2.15 million in bad debt after an exploiter manipulated the price of the Thena protocol's THE token. THE had very low liquidity, and the exploiter took advantage of it to manipulate the THE price oracle by borrowing against THE, using the borrowed funds to buy more THE, and repeating — causing the price oracle to reflect higher and higher prices. The attacker was able to avoid a supply cap on Venus by "donating" the funds rather than depositing them in the standard way.While the exploit left the Venus Protocol with over $2 million in bad debt, it's not clear if the attacker even made money from the exploit. The exploiter's position was ultimately liquidated, collapsing the increase in THE price. However, it's possible the exploiter took advantage of the price discrepancy elsewhere to profit.The Venus Protocol has had a number of issues in the past — notably in June 2023, when the team developing the BNB Chain had to intervene when the a thief borrowed $150 million on Venus against stolen tokens and then faced liquidation."Venus Protocol left with roughly $2M in bad debt after exploit manipulates Thena's THE token price", The BlockMarch 15, 2026BlockFills goes bankruptApproximately a month after halting deposits and withdrawals, citing liquidity issues and "recent market and financial conditions", the American crypto lender BlockFills has filed for bankruptcy. Filings in Delaware bankruptcy court reveal the company has between $50 million and $100 million in assets and between $100 million and $500 million in liabilities. The list of creditors include customers like 007 Capital and Artha Investment Partners, and the firm has a $4.75 million loan outstanding to fellow crypto lender Nexo. Also on the list of creditors are the Chicago Blackhawks, with whom BlockFills signed a sponsorship deal in 2022.BlockFills was backed by investors including Susquehanna and CME Ventures.Chapter 11 Voluntary Petition, Reliz Technology Group Holdings Inc.March 12, 2026Trader loses almost $50 million in Aave swap gone wrongA trader using the Aave interface attempted to swap $50 million USDT for AAVE. However, due to the enormous size of the order, the purchase had dramatic impact on the aave price. The Aave interface warned the customer about the price impact, and the trader clicked a checkbox to accept the order terms. Ultimately, they received only 324 AAVE (~$37,600) in return for their $50 million, losing 99.9% of their assets in the process.The Aave founder offered to refund the user the $600,000 in fees collected from the transaction, and acknowledged "there are additional guardrails the industry can build to better protect users".Tweet by Stani Kulechov [archive]Tweet by Martin Grabina [archive]No JavaScript? That's cool too! Check out the Web 1.0 version of the site to see more entries.

评论（默认中文）

post_break 2026/04/16 03:51
值得庆幸的是，App Store 不允许侧面加载，因为它完全阻止了这样的欺诈行为。至少这是我不断被告知如果我们允许侧面加载就会发生这种情况的首要原因。
Thankfully the App Store doesn't allow side loading, because it completely stops fraud like this. At least that's the number one reason why I keep getting told if we allow side loading this will happen.
在 HN 中查看
victorbjorklund 2026/04/16 04:24
App Store 或开放互联网上是否存在更多 web3 骗局？不是为苹果辩护，而是声称他们可以 100% 阻止欺诈和滥用行为，这有点像稻草人。这就像说安全带不起作用，因为人们仍然会在车祸中受伤。
Is there more scams of web3 in the App Store or on the open internet? Not defending Apple but kind of a strawman to claim they said it stops 100% of fraud and abuse. That’s like saying seatbelts don’t work because people still get hurt in car crashes.
在 HN 中查看
chocochunks 2026/04/16 05:01
App Store 是完全安全的，所以我不需要考虑我下载的内容或进行任何尽职调查！
The App Store is totally safe, so I don't need to think about what I download or do any due diligence!
在 HN 中查看
nathanmills 2026/04/16 07:35
难道你不认为仅仅比较存在并安装“经批准的”（苹果每年 100 美元）软件与安全带相比是稻草人吗？没有不系安全带的用例。对于能够安装软件而言，情况并非如此。
And don't you think its a strawman to compare only being and to install "" approved "" ($100/year for apple) software to a seatbelt? There is no use case for not wearing a seatbelt. That is not true for being able to install software.
在 HN 中查看
httgbgg 2026/04/16 10:04
很多人不同意不系安全带的情况。你发现无法想象，这实际上使它成为一个更好的类比。
Plenty of people disagree that there is no use case to not wearing a seatbelt. That you find it impossible to imagine makes it an even better analogy actually.
在 HN 中查看
array_key_first 2026/04/16 10:21
人们可以不同意任何事情，每个人都可以犯傻。

但大多数理性的人都认为，不系安全带没有任何实际用途。使用应用程序商店之外的软件有无数实际的用例，理性的人都可以承认这一点。

People can disagree with whatever, everyone is allowed to be stupid.

But most reasonable people agree there's no tangible use case to not wearing a seatbelt. There are infinite tangible use cases to using software outside the app store, that reasonable people can all acknowledge.

在 HN 中查看
idle_zealot 2026/04/16 05:09
呃，有点无力的论点。很容易用“但侧载会让这种情况发生更多！”来反驳。这甚至可能是正确的，而且数量上的差异很重要。毕竟，永远不会有一个完全安全的系统。

我认为真正的问题在于应用商店如何改变人们对软件的思考和联系方式。事实是，在您的计算机上运行代码 是 危险的。你 是 信任其对其运营的控制权。负责任的做法是提供平台级保护措施（权限系统、沙箱），并形成一种普遍的理解，即您应该只运行经过您将手机交给的人审核的应用程序。

当然，这从根本上与软件市场不相容，因此永远不会走这条路。

Eh, kinda a weak argument. Too easy to counter with "but sideloading would let that happen more!" That might even be right, and a difference in amount is important. There will never be a totally secure system, after all.

I think the actual problem is with how the App Store changes the way people think about and relate to software. The fact is, running code on your computer is dangerous. You are trusting it with control over its operations. The responsible thing to do is provide platform-level safeguards (permissions systems, sandboxing) and engender a general understanding that you should only run an app vetted by someone you would hand your phone to.

This is fundamentally incompatible with software as a market, of course, so this path will never be taken.

在 HN 中查看
throw1234567891 2026/04/16 04:47
如果他们这样做了，我们每天都会读到这样的案例。
If they did, we’d be reading about such cases daily.
在 HN 中查看
tadfisher 2026/04/16 03:46
文章来源： https://www.coindesk.com/business/2026/04/14/a-fake-ledger-a...

选择报价：

> 区块链调查员 ZachXBT 后来追踪到了被盗的 5.92 BTC [0]，显示它通过一系列交易迅速流入 KuCoin 存款地址，这与整个事件中发现的更广泛的洗钱模式一致。

啊，没有什么比一家位于塞舌尔的加密货币交易所更好的了，该交易所因洗钱活动而被美国禁止。这对比特币来说是件好事。

[0]: https://t.me/investigations/313#

Source article: https://www.coindesk.com/business/2026/04/14/a-fake-ledger-a...

Choice quote:

> Blockchain investigator ZachXBT later traced the stolen 5.92 BTC [0], showing it was rapidly funneled through a series of transactions into KuCoin deposit addresses, consistent with a broader laundering pattern identified across the incident.

Ah, there's nothing else quite like a Seychelles-based cryptocurrency exchange which was booted from the US for facilitating money laundering. This is good for Bitcoin.

[0]: https://t.me/investigations/313#

在 HN 中查看
hnburnsy 2026/04/16 05:16
因此，DUNS、电话号码和实际地址信息肯定会暴露肇事者，感谢苹果开发者注册。
So certainly the DUNS, phone number, and physical address information will give up the perpetrators, thank goodness for Apple developer registration.
在 HN 中查看
pixel_popping 2026/04/16 03:45
在手机上用那么多钱输入助记词真是毫无意义：/
Entering your seed phrase with that much money on a phone is really non-sense :/
在 HN 中查看
basilikum 2026/04/16 05:21
> 人们在应用程序中输入助记词，然后发现他们的钱包立即被掏空。

为什么他们立即兑现？将种子短语发送到服务器并在更长时间内不被发现，只收集种子短语直到一次性清除它们不是更聪明吗？

> people entered their seed phrases into the app, then discovered their wallets were immediately drained.

Why did they cash out immediately? Wouldn't it be much smarter to send the seed phrase to a server and stay undetected for longer just collecting seed phrases until you sweep them all at once?

在 HN 中查看
alasano 2026/04/16 05:28
也许他们进行了检查以确定所有收集的种子的总价值，然后从某个阈值触发自动扫描以保证最小值。

不知道博弈论最佳的偷窃方式是什么！

maybe they had a check to determine total value of all collected seeds and then triggered auto sweeps from a certain threshold to guarantee a minimum.

Not sure what the game theory optimal way of stealing is!

在 HN 中查看
basilikum 2026/04/16 05:39
这是有道理的。

但也许他们只是直接从应用程序到硬编码地址进行交易。不发出任何额外的网络请求可能会降低在应用商店审核过程中被自动化系统标记的机会。然后，您再次可以将这些请求伪装成普通的区块链连接。

我可能想多了，这只是一次快速而愚蠢的抢钱行为。

That would make sense.

But perhaps they just made a transaction directly from the app to a hardcoded address. Not making any additional network requests might decrease the chance of being flagged by automated systems in the Appstore review process. Then again you could just disguise these requests as ordinary block chain connections.

I'm probably over thinking this and it was just a quick and dumb money grab.

在 HN 中查看
basilikum 2026/04/16 05:33
网络犯罪的博弈论太有趣了。
Game theory of cybercrime is way too interesting.
在 HN 中查看
cank 2026/04/16 05:52
一鸟在手胜过十鸟在林。 “收集和清扫”策略风险极大，因为种子很容易腐烂。您正在与应用程序遭到攻击和用户轮换密钥进行竞赛。
a bird in the hand is worth much more than ten in the bush. The 'collect and sweep' strategy is extremely risky because seeds are perishable. You're racing against the app getting nuked and the users rotating keys.
在 HN 中查看
basilikum 2026/04/16 08:07
但人们真的会知道并轮换密钥吗？一旦应用程序被攻击，你就可以抢到所有的钱。
But would people actually know and rotate keys? The moment the app gets nuked you could grab all the money.
在 HN 中查看
hnburnsy 2026/04/16 05:18

dude250711 2026/04/16 03:06

Unidirectional wall garden.
在 HN 中查看
2OEH8eoCRo0 2026/04/16 03:42
苹果应该为此承担责任。

如果沃尔玛销售危险产品，即使在不知情的情况下，他们也可能要承担责任。为什么数字商店有所不同？

Apple should be liable for this.

If Walmart sells a dangerous product, even unknowingly, they can be liable. Why are digital stores different?

在 HN 中查看
alasano 2026/04/16 05:30
如果苹果对此负有责任，那么您可以使用自己的假冒应用程序从您拥有的大钱包中“窃取”钱，并让苹果赔偿您的“损失”，从而实现双重浸入。除了你的其他不义之财之外。
If apple were liable for this you could do a double dip where you use your own fake app to "steal" money from a massive wallet you own and get apple to pay you back for "your loss". In addition to your other ill gotten gains.
在 HN 中查看
xethos 2026/04/16 10:25
前提是你通过了苹果的开发者审查，这是他们最公开的论据，说明为什么软件必须首先经过苹果的审查。那么它们是否绝对正确（防止成为头条新闻并反驳你的论点），或者是否存在某个时间点，一些用户比苹果更了解，他们的开发者评论弊大于利，而苹果应用商店并不是苹果喜欢公开宣称的避风港？
Only if you made it past Apple's developer review, which is their most public argument for why software must be vetted by Apple first. So are they infallible (preventing the headline and refuting your argument), or is there a point in time when some users know better than Apple, when their developer review does as much harm as good, and the Apple App Store is not the safe haven Apple likes to publically claim?

nathanmills 2026/04/16 07:38

That's still fraud.
在 HN 中查看
alasano 2026/04/16 09:52

Of course it is.

pwillia7 2026/04/16 04:03

I thought that Apple was reviewing each and every app which was the reason that justified them getting a silly 30% margin from all app revenues?

pants2 2026/04/16 03:51

Apple only banned they app because they didn't get a 30% cut of the stolen crypto
在 HN 中查看
Ekaros 2026/04/16 04:48
我认为苹果生态系统没有糟糕的应用程序，因为它阻止了侧载。我在 HN 上多次听说过作为阻止它的理由。
I thought that Apple ecosystem had no bad apps as it prevented sideloading. I have heard that as reasoning to prevent it multiple times here on HN.
在 HN 中查看
armadyl 2026/04/16 05:00
“飞机失事了？瞧！美国联邦航空局的规定毫无用处，该机构需要解散。”
"A plane crashed? See! FAA regulations are useless and the agency needs to be disbanded."
在 HN 中查看
array_key_first 2026/04/16 10:25
不同之处在于，没有人说美国联邦航空局的规定使飞机失事不可能发生。

许多人认为苹果正在做的事情让恶意软件成为不可能。事实并非如此，应用程序商店中有大量恶意软件，并且在应用程序商店中获取恶意软件是微不足道的。

应用程序商店试图解决问题的子集的子集的子集的子集的子集，但它甚至没有解决这个问题。是的，不幸的是，这意味着它作为一个解决方案很糟糕。

或者，更明确地说：问题空间正在被欺骗。苹果只是想解决一个问题 非常小 该问题空间的一小部分。

您不需要应用程序来诈骗某人。如果您确实有一个应用程序，它不必位于应用程序商店之外。即使它位于应用程序商店中，也不一定是恶意的。

即使苹果以某种方式神奇地消除了恶意软件，仍然存在可用于诈骗的完全合法的应用程序。无论如何，这只能解决一小部分诈骗，因为绝大多数诈骗不是使用应用程序完成的。

The difference is nobody is saying that FAA regulations make plane crashes impossible.

Many people think what apple is doing makes malware impossible. That's not the case, the app store has plenty of malware and it's trivial to get malware on the app store.

The app store is trying to solve a subset of a subset of a subset of a subset of the problem, and then it doesn't even solve that. Yes, that means it sucks ass as a solution, unfortunately.

Or, to be more clear: the problem space is getting scammed. Apple is only even trying to solve an extraordinarily small subsection of that problem space.

You don't need an app to scam someone. And if you do have an app, it doesnt have to be outside the app store. And even if it's on the app store, it doesn't have to be malicious.

Even if apple did somehow, magically, eliminate malware there would still exist perfectly legitimate apps that can be used for scamming. And that would only address a tiny part of scamming anyway, because the vast majority of scams are not done using an app.

在 HN 中查看
2muchcoffeeman 2026/04/16 04:59
这是否意味着因为安全带和安全气囊的存在是为了在车祸中挽救我的生命，所以我应该不那么小心驾驶？

就像保暖一样，分层也是关键。

Does this mean that because seatbelts and air bags exist to save my life in a crash I should be less careful driving?

Like staying warm, it’s all about layers.

在 HN 中查看
xethos 2026/04/16 10:29
苹果对应用程序采取了积极主动的立场，这些应用程序甚至可以 用过的 访问成人内容。这就是 Tumblr 日子不好过的原因。

说仅此而已 能力 访问成人内容很可能会被关闭，但加密钱包很好，感觉像是双重标准

Apple takes a proactive stance towards apps that can even be used to access adult content. That's why Tumblr had a hard time.

Saying the mere ability to access adult content is very likely to get one shut down, but crypto wallets are fine, feels like a double standard

在 HN 中查看
wiseowise 2026/04/16 04:44
但你想过孩子吗？当合法的骗子无法从合法赌博（如战利品箱）中获得足够的现金时，上帝禁止他们向非法骗子提供资金。
But have you thought of children? God forbid they give money to illegitimate scammers when legitimate one aren’t getting enough cash from legal gambling like loot boxes.
在 HN 中查看
throw1234567891 2026/04/16 04:16

ThEY sHoUlD Pay AttENtIoN tO WhAt tHey aR3 d01n6!
在 HN 中查看
throw1234567891 2026/04/16 04:19 deleted
[已标记]
[flagged]
在 HN 中查看
irl_zebra 2026/04/16 04:05
这不应该发生。但我很难对加密货币人士表示同情。引用文章中的话：

“当我将 Ledger 转移到新电脑上并意外地从 Apple 商店下载了恶意分类账应用程序时，我在黑客/诈骗中损失了我的退休基金。我所有的 BTC 瞬间消失了。”

让我真的摇头。如果有人有知识购买比特币或加密货币，我想他们有足够的知识知道这种投机是多么的犯罪猖獗和风险。这就像有人决定将退休基金用于购买散装非法药物，然后以大幅加价出售。风险很大，潜在的上升空间很大，但考虑到他们评估并接受了风险，当他们被抢走所有药物并失去退休金时，很难感到难过。

This should not have happened. But I have a hard time finding any sympathy for cryptocurrency folks. The quote from the article:

"I lost my retirement fund in a hack/Scam when I switched my Ledger over to my new computer and by accident downloaded a malicious ledger app from the Apple store. All my BTC gone in an instant."

Leaves me really shaking my head. If someone has the knowledge to even buy bitcoin or cryptocurrency, I imagine they have enough knowledge to know how utterly crime-ridden and risky of a speculation it is. It's like if someone decides to put their retirement fund into buying bulk illegal drugs and then selling them at a massive markup. Pretty risky, potential high upside, but given they assessed and then accepted the risk, hard to feel bad when they get robbed of all their drugs and lose their retirement funds.

在 HN 中查看
tencentshill 2026/04/16 02:46
他们只需要它在应用程序商店中存在一周，然后就可以零追索权窃取数百万美元。 这些富有的加密货币人士需要停止贪图便宜并聘请财务顾问。不这样做的唯一原因是它首先是非法获得的。
They only needed it to exist on the app store for a week before stealing millions with zero recourse. These wealthy crypto people need to stop being cheap and hire financial advisors. The only reason for not doing so is if it was gained illegally in the first place.
在 HN 中查看
wmf 2026/04/16 03:41
很多人进入加密货币领域是因为他们想管理自己的资金。他们不会使用加密货币财务顾问。
A lot of people got into crypto because they want to manage their own money. They aren't going to use crypto financial advisors.

> A lot of people got into crypto because they want to manage their own money
